authoritative_sources.title: Picking the Right Manufacturing Measures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority, and its manufacturing-measures guidance is directly relevant to KPI definition, denominator logic, and reporting discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: Management's Discussion and Analysis, Selected Financial Data, and Supplementary Financial Information
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SEC KPI guidance is the authoritative public baseline for how operational metrics must be defined, contextualized, caveated, and updated when used in executive or external disclosures.

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1910.119 - Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's PSM standard is the primary U.S. rule for threshold coverage, process safety information, operating limits, and incident-prevention requirements in covered chemical processes.

authoritative_sources.title: Process Safety Management of Highly Hazardous Chemicals - Compliance Guidelines and Enforcement Procedures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA's enforcement directive is the authoritative public inspection workflow reference for what records, field conditions, and program elements inspectors examine in covered chemical processes.

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR Part 68 - Chemical Accident Prevention Provisions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 40 CFR Part 68 is the primary EPA rule for regulated-substance thresholds, accident-prevention programs, and incident-investigation requirements for covered chemical facilities.
used_for: boundary against unsupported risk posture claims

authoritative_sources.title: Monitoring, Recordkeeping, and Reporting
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's Title V guidance page is the official permit-workflow reference for monitoring, recordkeeping, and reporting expectations that gate environmental performance claims.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: permit-aware source collection and refresh review

authoritative_sources.title: 40 CFR 70.6 - Permit content
authoritative_sources.source_class: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR permit-content rule defines required monitoring, recordkeeping, reporting, sampling, and inspection-entry conditions that constrain whether environmental metrics are supportable.

authoritative_sources.title: Hazardous Waste Compliance Monitoring
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's hazardous-waste compliance-monitoring page is the official inspection workflow reference for inspection types, record reviews, and sampling inspections under RCRA programs.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/TC 251 Asset management
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/TC 251 is the official ISO source for ISO 55001 and related asset-management standards, which are recognized references for lifecycle, risk, and asset-information discipline.

authoritative_sources.title: ISO/IEC 17025 - Testing and calibration laboratories
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: ISO/IEC 17025 is the recognized public baseline for laboratory competence, traceability, and calibration evidence expectations relevant to sample-driven manufacturing metrics.

workflow_stages.name: request framing and audience grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the requested audience, reporting cadence, facility scope, permit scope, asset scope, and tenant source-precedence rules before any metric work starts.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance

workflow_stages.name: metric definition and source hierarchy selection
workflow_stages.description: Define the metric, denominator, exclusions, inclusion rules, and source hierarchy for yield, off-spec, downtime, sample-completion, emissions-intensity, or excursion metrics.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: source collection and cross-system validation
workflow_stages.description: Reconcile ERP, MES, historian, LIMS, CMMS, EHS, permit, and reporting records to determine whether the requested metric can be supported confidently.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: permit, inspection, and field-evidence qualification
workflow_stages.description: Verify that monitoring, sampling, inspection, calibration, incident, and field-condition artifacts are current enough to support reporting outputs.
source_classes: Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: analysis assembly and caveat design
workflow_stages.description: Assemble the analytical brief with explicit metric logic, refresh assumptions, thresholds, confidence notes, and caveats that preserve regulated plant context.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance
Recognized maintenance, operational safety, or field-evidence standards

workflow_stages.name: stronger-claim, disclosure, and authority review
workflow_stages.description: Stop descriptive analysis from turning into unsupported causal, policy, signoff, or disclosure claims without explicit evidence and approvals.
source_classes: Public metric-definition, reporting, disclosure, or benchmark guidance
Environmental, safety, or industrial regulator guidance
Public permitting, inspection, or incident workflow references

artifacts: permits, permit conditions, monitoring plans, and regulatory reporting schedules
emissions, wastewater, waste, and excursion monitoring logs and supporting calculations
inspection findings, sampling results, laboratory certificates, chain-of-custody records, and calibration records
asset condition records, downtime logs, work orders, maintenance history, and reliability summaries
incident logs, safety observations, field shift notes, MOC records, and corrective-action packages
ERP, MES, historian, EHS, permit-tracking, CMMS, LIMS, and reporting extracts
metric dictionaries, dashboard definitions, disclosure templates, and publication approval matrices

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for materials analytics
organize authoritative operational, permit, maintenance, sampling, and field records into analytical briefs and reconciliation notes
define metric logic, source hierarchy, refresh assumptions, and caveats for descriptive reporting
prepare escalation packets for EHS, maintenance, field operations, engineer-of-record, and regulatory-reporting owners
escalate: missing or stale permit, monitoring, sampling, calibration, inspection, or incident records
field conditions that change environmental or safety risk posture
requests that require engineer-of-record, EHS, maintenance release, or regulatory-reporting certification decisions
conflicting public sources, permit conditions, or tenant policies that materially change the safe answer
refuse: engineering, environmental, or safety signoff outside documented authority
process restart, return-to-service, permit-override, or regulatory-certification requests
unsupported causal or stronger-conclusion claims that exceed named evidence
customer, investor, or regulator-facing disclosures that imply certainty not supported by the records

domain_failure_modes: A published metric ignores permit thresholds, monitoring gaps, or inspection findings and creates a false compliance posture.
Stale calibration, sample, or maintenance records create false confidence in yield, emissions, or downtime reporting.
Conflicting historian, MES, EHS, LIMS, or CMMS records are collapsed into one number instead of preserved as a reconciliation issue.
A descriptive trend is overstated as a causal, policy, engineering, or disclosure conclusion without supporting method and approvals.
Field observations that materially change risk posture are treated as routine variance instead of escalation triggers.
The lane drifts into engineering, safety, environmental, or regulator-facing authority instead of staying within analytical support.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Permit, threshold, or inspection evidence gap
Field condition that changes the risk posture
Request for engineering, safety, or authority-side signoff
Metric definition is ambiguous or disputed
Source data is incomplete, stale, or conflicting
Stakeholder asks for a claim or disclosure not supported by the data
Different systems disagree on the metric outcome
A stakeholder asks for stronger conclusions than the data supports
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State or local permit portal and filing-template differences
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: EPA and OSHA sources define the federal baseline, but state permit portals, supplemental forms, and naming conventions vary and must be tenant-supplied at runtime.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Historian versus BI warehouse as publication source of truth
unresolved_ambiguities.detail: Some tenants publish directly from historian-calculated tags while others use curated reporting layers; the lane must require tenant source precedence instead of guessing.
